title: Document the idempotency contract in @moduledoc
prompt: Write a new worker module `MyApp.Workers.SendInvoiceEmailWorker`. Its `@moduledoc` must explicitly document that the worker is idempotent, that it tracks `sent_at` on the invoice record to short-circuit retries, and that Oban may call it more than once per logical invoice. The perform/1 accepts `%{"invoice_id" => id}`, checks `invoice.sent_at`, and returns `:ok` without re-sending if already set. Otherwise send the email via `MyApp.Mailer.send_invoice(invoice)` and call `MyApp.Billing.mark_sent(invoice)`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/send_invoice_email_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Canonical greenfield worker with an explicit contract statement — tests that the philosophy framing shows up in the module's own docs.
research_citation: Jonny Eberhardt, 'Keep Calm and Let Oban Handle Your Elixir Background Jobs' — 'Design your workers so that executing the same job multiple times does not produce adverse effects.'
